task_completion.description: Open the SRS document in the requirements management application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SRS document is visible in the main view of the application (e.g., ReqView).

task_completion.description: Locate requirement SRS-6 in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A row or item with the ID 'SRS-6' (or ID 6) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Update SRS-6 to contain only the encryption requirement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text for SRS-6 reads 'The system shall encrypt all sensitive user data stored locally using AES-256 encryption.' The word 'transmit' is no longer present in the SRS-6 text.

task_completion.description: Create a new requirement immediately following SRS-6.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new requirement row is visible directly below SRS-6 in the document hierarchy or list.

task_completion.description: Set the text of the newly created requirement to the transmission requirement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text for the new requirement reads 'The system shall transmit all data to the backend server using TLS 1.3 or higher.' The term 'AES-256' is not present in this new requirement.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's graphical user interface to edit the requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the ReqView application window, clicking on rows, and typing in text fields, rather than editing a JSON file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', or python scripts being executed to modify 'documents/SRS.json' directly.

integrity.description: The new requirement is placed adjacently to SRS-6.
integrity.visual_evidence: The new requirement is visually located immediately after SRS-6 in the document tree or list, not appended to the very bottom of an unrelated section.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target requirement ID is 6 (SRS-6). The updated SRS-6 MUST contain 'encrypt all sensitive user data stored locally using AES-256 encryption' and MUST NOT contain the word 'transmit'. The newly created requirement MUST contain 'transmit all data to the backend server using TLS 1.3 or higher' and MUST NOT contain 'AES-256'.
